steps.title: Use the one-solution condition
steps.content:
A quadratic equation has exactly one real solution when its discriminant is $0$. For
$$
-9x^2-12x+c=0
$$
the coefficients are $a=-9$ and $b=-12$.

steps.title: Solve for the constant
steps.content: $$ 36c=-144 $$ Divide both sides by $36$. The answer is -4.
